feedback.text: Hello! What is happening right now in the digital world is unacceptable. Huge corporates are trying to censor and push forward surveillance, while destroying products the costumers already paid for. Payment processors are now deciding what media is okay to platform and what isn't. Overnight over 27000 games have been removed from Steam because of it. The internet protection acts that are happening in the UK and US right now are going to force surveillance on everyone. Today its the UK, tomorrow its the US and next week its going to be for the whole world. I know its a lot, but its a multifaceted issue. We as costumers are being attacked from multiple angles at once, and i fear its only the beginning. Right now, what we need is a strong stance against costumer abuse. We need strong privacy laws and costumer protection acts, so that what we buy as costumers couldn't be taken away. And costumers should not be coerced to spend money on a game. - False urgency advertising: pop up windows that give you a one time purchase or a restricted time window to buy a bundle in a game. - Gambling-like features: spending money for random rewards. Even if they hide the random rewards behind an in-game currency, its still spending money for random rewards, since you can buy the in-game currency with real money. - Unsubscribe: If i dont want to get news mails from a company, it should be easy to say no. A click of a button. But lot of times, it doesnt work, and sometimes there isn't even a button. - Commercials should be readily understood: Social media content creators should highlight if they are sponsored by a company. Like badges in the F1. - Destroyed media: If a company decides to stop supporting a product, they should give the costumers the ability to support it themselves. For example: If Sony decides to end Helldivers 2 and shut down all servers for the game, they should give the costumers the ability to host servers for themselves. - False advertising: I see advertisements of mobile games every day that either got nothing to do with the game, or it is only a fraction of what the game is. They are showing me an interactive game design, meanwhile that is actually like 5% of the game and the other 95% is a click and wait design for which they give you the ability to skip if you pay real money (in-game currency that you can buy with real money). These are only a few ways costumers are being abused. We need an act and we need it now, before its too late. Thank you!
